Typical load: 5-15 concurrent\n223\t            requests during peak dispatch periods.\n224\t\n225\t            max_connections (150): Maximum concurrent connections to the proxy.\n226\t remain in pool.\n236\t                - 3-minute TTL balances reuse vs connection freshness\n237\t                - Spans multiple typical request cycles (router: ~2s, fetch: ~5-10s, synthesize: ~3-8s)\n238\t                - Reduces reconnection overhead during active sessions\n239\t\n240\t        These settings work together with HTTP/2 to minimize latency:\n241\t            - Connection reuse eliminates TCP+TLS setup (typically 200-500ms saved)\n242\t            - HTTP/2 multiplexing allows concurrent requests on single connection\n243\t            - Large pool handles connection-per-host edge cases\n244\t        \"\"\"\n245\t        if self._client is None:\n246\t            # Configure aggressive connection pooling for reduced latency\n247\t            # - HTTP/2       Make a single-turn LLM call.\n351\t\n352\t        Args:\n353\t            request: The LLM request\n354\t\n355\t        Returns:\n356\t            LLMResponse with content, usage info, and logger.debug(\n425\t                f\"LLM response: input_tokens={input_tokens}, output_tokens={output_tokens}, \"\n426\t                f\"timing_total_ms={total_ms:.2f}, Use SONNET for router despite HAIKU\n    being marketed as \"fast\". SONNET provides better price-performance for this use case.\n    \"\"\"\n    HAIKU = \"claude-haiku-4-20250514\"  # Fast, cheap - but slower for routing in practice\n    SONNET = \"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\"  # Balanced - network).\"\"\"\n        if self.timing_total_ms is not None and self.timing_network_ms is not None:\n            return max(0, self.timing_total_ms - self.timing_network_ms)\n        return None\n\n\nclass LLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for LLM errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass LLMTimeoutError(LLMError):\n    \"\"\"LLM request timed out.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass LLMRateLimitError(LLMError):\n    \"\"\"LLM rate limited.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass ZAIClient:\n    \"\"\"\n    Client for ZAI proxy (llm-proxy.ardenone.com).\n\n    Provides single-turn, stateless LLM calls for:\n    - Typical load: 5-15 concurrent\n            requests during peak dispatch periods.\n\n            max_connections (150): Maximum concurrent connections to the proxy.\n                - Sufficient for parallel fetch + synthesize operations\n                - Handles burst traffic from multi-intent utterances\n                - Prevents pool exhaustion under high concurrency\n\n            max_keepalive_connections (50): Connections kept alive for reuse.\n                - Reduces TLS handshake overhead (major latency factor)\n                - Enables connection reuse across request batches\n                - Complements HTTP/2 multiplexing efficiency\n\n            keepalive_expiry (180s): How long idle connections remain in pool.\n                - 3-minute TTL balances reuse vs connection freshness\n                - Spans multiple typical request cycles (router: ~2s, fetch: ~5-10s, synthesize: ~3-8s)\n                - Reduces reconnection overhead during active sessions\n\n        These settings work together with HTTP/2 to minimize latency:\n            - Connection reuse eliminates TCP+TLS setup (typically 200-500ms saved)\n            - HTTP/2 multiplexing allows concurrent requests on single connection\n            - Large pool handles connection-per-host edge cases\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._client is None:\n            # Configure {e}\")\n\n    async def call(self, request: LLMRequest) -> LLMResponse:\n        \"\"\"\n        Make a single-turn LLM call.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The LLM request\n\n        Returns:\n            LLMResponse with content, usage info, and timing breakdown\n\n        Raises:\n            LLMTimeoutError: If request times out\n            LLMRateLimitError: If rate limited\n            LLMError: For other errors\n        \"\"\"\n        import time\n        client = await self._get_client()\n\n        request_start_ms = time.perf_counter() * 1000\n        first_byte_ms =   # Use streaming to measure actual first-byte time (network latency)\n            async with client.stream(\n                \"POST\",\n                self.proxy_url,\n                json=payload,\n                headers={\n                    \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n                },\n            ) as response:\n                # Check for rate limit\n                if response.status_code == 429:\n                    raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\")\n\n                response.raise_for_status()\n\n                # Measure first-byte full response body (required for streaming responses)\n                response_text = await response.aread()\n                import json as json_lib\n                data = json_lib.loads(response_text)\n\n            request_end_ms = time.perf_counter() * 1000\n\n            # Log HTTP/2 usage for this request\n            http_version = getattr(response, 'http_version', 'unknown')\n            pool = client._transport._pool\n            protocol = \"HTTP/2\" if getattr(pool, '_http2', False) else \"HTTP/1.1\"\n            logger.debug(f\"LLM request total_ms = request_end_ms - request_start_ms\n            inference_ms = total_ms - network_ms\n\n            logger.debug(\n                f\"LLM response: input_tokens={input_tokens}, from e\n\n    async def call_simple(\n        self,\n        system_prompt: str,\n        user_message: str,\n        model: str | None = None,\n        max_tokens: int = 4096,\n        temperature: for the LLM\n            user_message: User message\n            model: Model to use (defaults to client's default_model)\n            max_tokens: Maximum tokens to generate\n            temperature: and timing breakdown\n        \"\"\"\n        request = LLMRequest(\n            system_prompt=system_prompt,\n            user_message=user_message,\n            model=model or self.default_model,\n      temperature: float = 0.7,\n    ) -> AsyncGenerator[Union[str, Dict[str, Any]], None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Stream LLM response for progressive card fill.\n\n        Yields text chunks as they arrive from the API.\n\n        Args:\n            system_prompt: System prompt for the LLM\n            user_message: User message\n            model: Model to use (defaults to client's default_model)\n            max_tokens: Maximum tokens to generate\n            temperature: Sampling temperature\n\n        Yields:\n            Text chunks as they arrive\n\n        Returns:\n            Complete response text and usage info in the final iteration\n        \"\"\"\n        request = LLMRequest(\n            system_prompt=system_prompt,\n            user_message=user_message,\n            model=model or self.default_model,\n            max_tokens=max_tokens,\n            temperature=temperature,\n        )\n\n        client = await self._get_client()\n\n        try:\n            payload = request.to_payload()\n            payload[\"stream\"] = True  # Enable streaming\n\n            logger.debug(f\"LLM streaming request: model={request.model}\")\n\n            async with client.stream(\n                \"POST\",\n                self.proxy_url,\n                json=payload,\n                headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"},\n            ) as response:\n                # Check for rate limit\n                if response.status_code == 429:\n                    raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\")\n\n                response.raise_for_status()\n\n                accumulated_text = []\n                input_tokens = 0\n                output_tokens = 0\n\n                # Process server-sent events\n                async for line in response.aiter_lines():\n                    if not line.strip():\n                        continue\n\n                    if line.startswith(\"data: \"):\n              }\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError as e:\n            raise LLMTimeoutError(f\"LLM streaming request timed out after {self.timeout}s\") from e\n        except httpx.TimeoutException requests are latency-sensitive and need rapid failure detection.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a dedicated router client with optimized settings\n    return ZAIClient(\n        proxy_url=proxy_url,\n startup.\n\n    Should be called in the application lifespan manager to establish connections\n    before the first user request arrives. test_client_has_connection_limits_configured(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify ZAI client has proper httpx.Limits configuration.\"\"\"\n        client = ZAIClient()\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        # Verify connection limits are properly configured via the pool\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n        assert hasattr(pool, \"_max_keepalive_connections\"), \"Pool should have keepalive connections limit\"\n        assert hasattr(pool, \"_max_connections\"), \"Pool should have max connections limit\"\n\n        # Check the actual limit values\n        assert pool._max_keepalive_connections >= 50, \"Keepalive connections should be >= 50\"\n        assert pool._max_connections >= 150, \"Max connections should be >= 150\"\n\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_pool_size_for_concurrent_requests(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify pool size is appropriate for handling concurrent requests.\"\"\"\n        client = ZAIClient()\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        # Check pool can handle concurrent requests\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n        assert hasattr(pool, \"_max_connections\"), \"Pool should have max connections attribute\"\n        assert hasattr(pool, \"_max_keepalive_connections\"), \"Pool should have max keepalive connections\"\n\n        # Pool should support at least 150 concurrent connections\n        assert pool._max_connections >= 150, \"Pool should support >= 150 concurrent connections\"\n\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_connection_reuse_across_requests(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify connection pool can handle multiple sequential requests.\"\"\"\n        client = ZAIClient()\n\n        # Track connection pool state\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n\n        # Verify pool is properly configured for connection reuse\n        assert pool._max_keepalive_connections >= 50, \"Should have large keepalive pool for reuse\"\n        assert pool._max_connections >= 150, \"Should have large max connections\"\n\n        # Connection reuse happens internally in httpx - we verify the pool is sized correctly\n        # Actual connection reuse is tested through integration tests with real connections\n\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_concurrent_dispatch_handling(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify connection pool handles concurrent dispatches correctly.\"\"\"\n        client = ZAIClient()\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        # Create multiple concurrent requests\n        async def make_request(request_id: int):\n            \"\"\"Simulate a concurrent request.\"\"\"\n            with patch.object(http_client, \"request\") as mock_request:\n                mock_response = MagicMock()\n                mock_response.status_code = 200\n                mock_response.headers = {}\n                mock_response.http_version = \"HTTP/2\"\n                mock_response.areturn = AsyncMock(return_value=b'{\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": \"test\"}]}}')\n                mock_request.return_value.__aenter__.return_value = mock_response\n\n                try:\n                    await client.call(LLMRequest(\n                        system_prompt=\"test\",\n                        user_message=f\"concurrent request {request_id}\"\n                    ))\n                    return f\"success-{request_id}\"\n                except Exception as e:\n                    return f\"error-{request_id}: {e}\"\n\n        # Launch 10 concurrent requests\n        tasks = [make_request(i) for i in range(10)]\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)\n\n        # All requests should complete without pool exhaustion\n        successful = [r for r in results if isinstance(r, str) and r.startswith(\"success-\")]\n        assert len(successful) >= 10, f\"All concurrent requests should succeed, got {len(successful)}/10\"\n\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_router_client_has_aggressive_pooling(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify router client has more aggressive connection pooling.\"\"\"\n        router_client = get_router_zai_client(timeout=8.0)\n        http_client = await router_client._get_client()\n\n        # Router client should have optimized pooling for low latency\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n\n        # Should have aggressive keepalive for fast repeated requests\n        assert pool._max_keepalive_connections >= 50, \"Router should have large keepalive pool\"\n        assert pool._max_connections >= 150, \"Router should handle concurrent requests\"\n\n        await router_client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_pool_configuration_documentation(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify pool settings are documented in code.\"\"\"\n        client = ZAIClient()\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        # Check that pool has reasonable defaults documented in code\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n\n        # These values should match the documented settings in src/escalate/llm.py\n        assert pool._max_keepalive_connections == 50, \"Keepalive should match documented value\"\n        assert pool._max_connections == 150, \"Max connections should match documented value\"\n\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_timeout_configuration_with_pooling(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify timeout configuration works well with connection pooling.\"\"\"\n        client = ZAIClient()\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        # Check timeout configuration\n        timeout_config = http_client._timeout\n\n        # Verify aggressive timeouts for fail-fast behavior\n        assert timeout_config.connect <= 8.0, \"Connect timeout should be aggressive\"\n        assert timeout_config.write <= 8.0, \"Write timeout should be aggressive\"\n        assert timeout_config.pool <= 3.0, \"Pool timeout should be aggressive\"\n\n        # These timeouts should complement the pooling strategy (keepalive is in pool)\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n        # Keepalive expires in 180s which is much longer than connect timeout - good for reuse\n        assert pool._max_keepalive_connections >= 50, \"Should maintain large keepalive pool\"\n\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_keepalive_settings_for_connection_reuse(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify keepalive settings promote connection reuse.\"\"\"\n        client = ZAIClient()\n        http_client = await client._get_client()\n\n        pool = http_client._transport._pool\n\n        # Pool should be configured for Create multiple concurrent requests\n        async def make_request(request_id: int):\n            \"\"\"Simulate a concurrent request.\"\"\"\n            with patch.object(http_client, \"request\") as mock_request:\n                mock_response = MagicMock()\n                mock_response.status_code = 200\n                mock_response.headers = {}\n                mock_response.http_version = \"HTTP/2\"\n                mock_response.areturn = AsyncMock(return_value=b'{\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": \"test\"}]}}')\n                mock_request.return_value.__aenter__.return_value = mock_response\n\n                try:\n                    await client.call(LLMRequest(\n                        system_prompt=\"test\",\n                        user_message=f\"concurrent request {request_id}\"\n                    ))\n                    return f\"success-{request_id}\"\n                except Exception as e:\n                    return f\"error-{request_id}: {e}\"\n\n        # Launch 10 concurrent requests\n        tasks = [make_request(i) for i in range(10)]\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)\n\n        # All requests should complete without pool exhaustion\n        successful = [r for r in results if isinstance(r, str) and r.startswith(\"success-\")]\n        assert len(successful) >= 10, f\"All concurrent requests should succeed, got {len(successful)}/10\"\n\n        await client.close()\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_router_client_has_aggressive_pooling(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify router client has more aggressive connection pooling.\"\"\"\n        router_client = get_router_zai_client(timeout=8.0)\n        http_client = await router_client._get_client()\n\n        #
